question: What is the primary election system in Delaware?
question_variants: Are Delaware primaries open or closed?
Do I have to register with a party to vote in the DE primary?
Can unaffiliated voters participate in the Delaware primary?
answer: Delaware has a strictly closed primary system. Voters must be officially registered as a member of the Democratic or Republican party to vote in that respective party's primary election. Unaffiliated or independent voters cannot participate in partisan primary elections.

question: What is the candidate filing deadline for major party candidates in Delaware for 2026?
question_variants: When do candidates file for office in Delaware?
Delaware filing deadline 2026
July filing deadline DE
answer: Because Delaware holds one of the latest primaries in the nation, its filing deadline is correspondingly late. Major party candidates must file their notification of candidacy by the second Tuesday in July. For the 2026 cycle, the deadline is July 14, 2026, at 12:00 noon.

question: Are write-in candidates permitted in Delaware?
question_variants: Do write-in votes count in DE?
How to register as a write-in candidate in Delaware
Write-in filing deadline Delaware 2026
answer: Write-in candidates are not permitted in primary elections. To have write-in votes tabulated in the general election, a candidate must officially file a Declaration of Intent as a write-in candidate with the State Election Commissioner no later than September 20 of the election year.

question: What is the petition signature threshold for independent candidates in Delaware?
question_variants: Independent candidate ballot access Delaware
Running without a party in DE 2026
How many signatures for independent US Senate DE
answer: Independent candidates must submit a nominating petition signed by registered voters equal to at least 1% of the total number of registered voters in the state (or district, for non-statewide offices) as of December 31 of the preceding year. The petition and sworn declaration must be filed by September 1 of the election year.

question: Does Delaware have a sore loser law?
question_variants: Can I run as an independent if I lose the DE primary?
Delaware sore loser statute
General election ballot access after primary defeat DE
answer: Yes. Delaware enforces a sore loser law. A candidate who runs unsuccessfully for a political party's nomination in the primary election is prohibited from subsequently filing to run as an independent candidate or a write-in candidate for the same office in the ensuing general election.

question: Do the Governor and Lieutenant Governor run on a joint ticket in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware joint ticket rules
How are Lt Gov candidates selected in DE?
Does the Governor pick a running mate in Delaware?
answer: No. In Delaware, the Governor and Lieutenant Governor are elected completely separately. They run in separate primary and general elections and appear as separate items on the ballot, meaning voters can (and occasionally do) elect a Governor and Lieutenant Governor from two different political parties.

question: Does Delaware hold primary runoff elections?
question_variants: What is the threshold to win a primary in DE?
Does Delaware require a majority to win a primary?
Delaware primary runoff rules
answer: No. Delaware does not hold primary runoff elections. A candidate does not need to secure a 50%+1 majority to win the party's nomination; the candidate who receives a simple plurality (the highest number) of the votes in the primary advances directly to the general election.

question: When are the primary and general elections in Delaware for 2026?
question_variants: Delaware primary date 2026
When is Election Day 2026 in Delaware?
September primary Delaware
answer: Delaware holds one of the latest primaries in the nation. The 2026 state and federal primary election will be held on Tuesday, September 15, 2026. The general election will be held on Tuesday, November 3, 2026. Polls are open from 7:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m.

question: Is the Delaware Governor running for election in 2026?
question_variants: Delaware Governor election 2026
Who is defending the DE Governor seat?
Is Matt Meyer on the 2026 ballot?
answer: No. Delaware elects its Governor and Lieutenant Governor exclusively on the presidential election cycle. Governor Matt Meyer (D) was elected in November 2024, and his four-year term runs through early 2029. There is no gubernatorial race on the 2026 midterm ballot.

question: Which statewide executive offices are up for election in Delaware in 2026?
question_variants: Is the Attorney General up in 2026?
DE statewide election list 2026
When is the State Treasurer election in DE?
answer: While the Governor is not on the ballot, Delaware elects several key statewide executives on the midterm cycle. In 2026, the offices of Attorney General, State Auditor of Accounts, and State Treasurer are all on the ballot for four-year terms.

question: Which U.S. Senate seat is up for election in Delaware in 2026?
question_variants: Is Chris Coons up for re-election in 2026?
When is Lisa Blunt Rochester's next election?
Delaware US Senate cycle 2026
answer: The Class 2 U.S. Senate seat currently held by Chris Coons (D) is up for election in 2026. Senator Lisa Blunt Rochester (Class 1) was elected to the Senate in 2024 and will not appear on the ballot again until 2030.

question: How many U.S. House districts are in Delaware for the 2026 election?
question_variants: Delaware congressional apportionment
Are all DE congressional seats up in 2026?
Number of US Representatives from Delaware
answer: Delaware is apportioned exactly 1 U.S. House district. The entire state is represented by a single At-Large district, which is on the 2026 ballot for a two-year term.

question: Which Delaware State Legislature seats are up for election in 2026?
question_variants: Are all Delaware House seats up in 2026?
Delaware Senate election cycle 2026
Which legislative districts are on the ballot in DE?
answer: All 41 seats in the Delaware House of Representatives (2-year terms) are up for election in 2026. The Delaware State Senate has 21 members who serve 4-year staggered terms; approximately half the chamber (10 or 11 seats, depending on the post-redistricting cohort cycle) will be on the 2026 ballot.

question: Does Delaware place constitutional amendments on the ballot for voters?
question_variants: Delaware ballot measure schedule 2026
When do citizens vote on DE constitutional amendments?
Delaware unique ballot measure rules
answer: No. In a major constitutional distinctive, Delaware is the ONLY state in the nation where the state constitution can be amended without a popular vote by the citizenry. The General Assembly amends the constitution directly by passing the amendment with a two-thirds vote in two consecutive legislative sessions. Therefore, there are no mandatory ballot measures for amendments.

question: What is the schedule for the Delaware General Assembly sessions?
question_variants: When does the DE legislature meet?
Delaware legislative session length
Is the Delaware legislature full time?
answer: The Delaware General Assembly is a part-time legislature that meets annually. The regular session convenes on the second Tuesday in January and, by constitutional mandate, must conclude its business by June 30 of each year.

question: What are the eligibility requirements to run for Governor of Delaware?
question_variants: Age and residency for Delaware Governor
Delaware Constitution Governor qualifications
Who is eligible to be Governor of DE?
answer: Under Article III of the Delaware Constitution, a candidate for Governor must be at least 30 years old, a citizen of the United States for at least 12 years, and a resident of Delaware for at least six years immediately preceding the election.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Candidate Qualifications

question: What are the qualifications to run for the Delaware State Legislature?
question_variants: Qualifications for DE State Representative
Age requirement Delaware State Senate
Do I have to live in my district to run in DE?
answer: Under Article II of the Delaware Constitution, a candidate for the State Senate must be at least 27 years old, and a State House candidate must be at least 24 years old. Both must be citizens of Delaware for at least three years, and residents of the specific district for at least one year immediately preceding the election.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Candidate Qualifications

question: What are the eligibility requirements for U.S. House and Senate candidates in Delaware?
question_variants: US Senate residency requirements Delaware
Age to run for Congress in Delaware
Federal constitutional requirements for DE candidates
answer: Federal candidates must meet standard U.S. Constitutional requirements: U.S. House members must be at least 25 years old, a U.S. citizen for 7 years, and an inhabitant of the state when elected. U.S. Senators must be at least 30 years old, a U.S. citizen for 9 years, and an inhabitant of the state when elected.

question: What are the term limits for Delaware state officials?
question_variants: How many terms can a Delaware Governor serve?
Are there term limits for the Delaware legislature?
Delaware executive term limit law
answer: Under the Delaware Constitution, the Governor is strictly limited to serving two consecutive four-year terms. However, there are NO term limits for members of the Delaware General Assembly (State House or State Senate).

question: Does the Delaware Attorney General have to be a licensed attorney?
question_variants: Qualifications to run for Attorney General in DE
Do I need to be a member of the bar to be DE AG?
Legal requirements for Delaware Attorney General
answer: Yes. Statutory law requires the Attorney General of Delaware to be a qualified elector (registered voter) of the state and a licensed attorney authorized to practice law in Delaware.

question: What are the qualifications to run for Lieutenant Governor in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware Lt Gov requirements
Does the Lt Gov have the same requirements as the Governor in DE?
Qualifications for Lieutenant Governor of Delaware
answer: Under Article III of the Delaware Constitution, the qualifications for Lieutenant Governor are identical to those for Governor. A candidate must be at least 30 years old, a U.S. citizen for 12 years, and a Delaware resident for 6 years.

question: What are the eligibility requirements for county officeholders in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware county council qualifications
Can I run for Levy Court in DE?
County residency for DE officials
answer: To run for a county office (such as County Council in New Castle, or Levy Court in Kent/Sussex counties), a candidate must generally be a qualified elector, at least 24 years old (varies by county), a resident of the county, and a resident of the specific district they seek to represent.

question: Is a strict photo ID required to vote in person in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware voter ID requirements
Do I need my driver's license to vote in DE?
Acceptable ID for Delaware voting
answer: No. Delaware does not have a strict photo ID requirement. Voters are asked to present a valid ID at the polls, which can be a photo ID (like a driver's license) OR a non-photo document (like a utility bill or paycheck). If a voter lacks ID entirely, they can still cast a regular ballot by signing an affidavit affirming their identity.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Voter ID

question: Is Delaware a universal mail ballot state, and is no-excuse absentee voting allowed?
question_variants: Do all voters get a mail ballot in Delaware?
Delaware vote-by-mail system
Do I need an excuse to vote absentee in DE?
answer: No. Delaware is not a universal mail ballot state. Historically, the state constitution restricted absentee voting to six specific excuses. While the legislature passed constitutional amendments in 2026 to enshrine no-excuse absentee voting, the process requires passage across two legislative sessions. Currently, voters must still meet specific statutory criteria (like illness, disability, or business absence) to vote absentee.

question: Does Delaware offer early in-person voting?
question_variants: Can I vote early in person in DE?
Delaware early voting dates 2026
When does early voting start in Delaware?
answer: Yes. Delaware law permits 10 days of early in-person voting prior to primary, general, and special elections. While this statutory provision was briefly struck down by a lower court in 2024, the Delaware Supreme Court reversed the decision, keeping the 10-day early voting period fully operative for the 2026 cycle.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections / General Assembly

question: What is the voter registration deadline and does Delaware offer same-day registration?
question_variants: Last day to register to vote in DE
Delaware same day voter registration
Voter registration deadline 2026 Delaware
answer: The deadline to register to vote in Delaware is the fourth Saturday prior to an election (October 10 for the 2026 general election, and August 22 for the primary). A previous law attempting to establish same-day registration was struck down by the Delaware Supreme Court in 2022, so voters MUST register prior to the deadline.

question: What is the electioneering buffer zone distance in Delaware?
question_variants: How close can I campaign to a polling place in DE?
Delaware 50 feet electioneering rule
DE buffer zone around voting centers
answer: Under Delaware Code Title 15 § 4942, electioneering is strictly prohibited within the polling place or within 50 feet of the entrance to the building in which the voting room is located. This bans distributing campaign literature or wearing apparel referring to candidates or partisan topics.

question: How are voting rights restored for individuals with felony convictions in Delaware?
question_variants: Can felons vote in Delaware state?
Felony voting rights restoration DE
Voting on parole in Delaware
answer: In Delaware, most individuals convicted of a felony have their voting rights automatically restored upon the full completion of their sentence, including all periods of incarceration, probation, and parole. However, individuals convicted of certain disqualifying felonies (like murder, manslaughter, or certain sexual offenses) permanently lose their voting rights unless pardoned.

question: How many counties are in Delaware, and how does this affect election administration?
question_variants: Delaware county election administration
How many county boards in DE?
Local election control Delaware
answer: Delaware has only 3 counties: New Castle, Kent, and Sussex. The state Department of Elections maintains centralized oversight, but operates a designated County Department of Elections office in each of the three counties to conduct voting logistics and process absentee ballots.

question: How can campaigns access the Delaware voter file?
question_variants: Delaware voter database access cost
How to get the voter list in DE
What data is included in the Delaware voter file?
answer: Campaigns, political committees, and candidates can request the statewide or county-specific voter registration list directly from the Department of Elections. The list contains voter names, addresses, party affiliation, and voting history. Usage is restricted exclusively to political and election-related purposes.

question: Which agency administers campaign finance tracking in Delaware?
question_variants: Where do I file campaign finance reports in DE?
What is the Delaware Public Integrity Commission?
Does the DE Election Commissioner handle campaign finance?
answer: In a crucial accuracy point, while the Public Integrity Commission handles ethics and lobbying, Campaign Finance in Delaware is administered strictly by the State Election Commissioner via the Department of Elections' online Campaign Finance Reporting System (CFRS).
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Campaign Finance

question: What are the individual campaign contribution limits in Delaware for the 2026 cycle?
question_variants: Are there contribution limits in Delaware?
DE individual donation caps 2026 election
Maximum donation to state legislature Delaware
answer: Delaware enforces per-election-period contribution limits. An individual may contribute a maximum of $1,200 to a candidate for statewide office, and $600 to a candidate for non-statewide office (such as State Senate or House). Because the primary and general are separate periods, donors can give the maximum for the primary and again for the general.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Contribution Table

question: Are corporate contributions permitted in Delaware campaigns?
question_variants: Corporate contribution ban Delaware state
Can a business donate to my campaign in DE?
Corporate campaign finance rules Delaware
answer: Yes. Direct contributions from the general treasury of a corporation, LLC, or labor union to a candidate's campaign committee are permitted in Delaware. They are subject to the exact same contribution limits as individual donors ($1,200 statewide, $600 non-statewide).
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Contribution Limits

question: What is the general campaign finance reporting schedule for Delaware candidates?
question_variants: DE CFRS filing dates 2026
When are campaign finance reports due in DE?
Delaware pre-primary and pre-general report deadlines
answer: Delaware requires an annual Year-End report due by December 31. During an election year in which the candidate is on the ballot, the committee must also file a 30-Day Pre-Election Report and an 8-Day Pre-Election Report. (Pending legislation aimed at implementing quarterly reporting takes effect in 2028).

question: Does Delaware provide public financing for state campaigns?
question_variants: Public matching funds for Delaware Governor
State funded campaigns Delaware status
Government campaign grants DE
answer: No. Delaware does not have a system of public financing or matching funds for state-level political campaigns. Candidates must rely entirely on private contributions raised within the statutory limits.

question: Are candidates prohibited from using campaign funds for personal use in Delaware?
question_variants: DE personal use prohibition campaign finance
Can I use campaign money for personal items in DE?
Prohibited expenditures Delaware CFRS
answer: Yes. Under Delaware campaign finance laws, campaign funds cannot be converted to personal use. All expenditures must be reasonably related to the election campaign or the execution of the duties of public office.

question: Are there limits on a candidate loaning personal funds to their own campaign in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware candidate personal loan limits
Can I pay myself back for a campaign loan in DE?
Reporting candidate loans in CFRS
answer: No. A candidate may loan or contribute an UNLIMITED amount of their own personal funds to their campaign committee; these funds are exempt from standard contribution limits. Loans must be properly documented on disclosures and may be repaid from campaign contributions raised during the cycle.

question: What are the PAC contribution limits to candidates in Delaware?
question_variants: DE PAC donation limits to candidates 2026
Political committee vs individual contribution limit Delaware
Maximum PAC gift to state legislator DE
answer: In Delaware, Political Action Committees (PACs) are subject to the EXACT SAME contribution limits as individuals and corporations. For the 2025-2026 cycle, a PAC is capped at donating $1,200 per election period to a candidate for statewide office, and $600 to a legislative candidate.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Contribution Limits

question: Who draws Delaware's congressional and state legislative maps?
question_variants: Does the DE legislature draw district maps?
Who has the authority to draw district lines in Delaware?
Delaware redistricting commission
answer: In Delaware, the state legislature (General Assembly) holds the exclusive authority to draw and enact both the single congressional district and the state legislative district boundaries. The maps are passed as regular legislation and are subject to the Governor's signature or veto.

question: What are the legal criteria for redistricting in Delaware?
question_variants: Standards for drawing Delaware district lines
Delaware gerrymandering rules
Map requirements DE
answer: Under the Delaware Constitution and state statutes, districts must be contiguous, be bounded by major roads or natural features when possible, and be as nearly equal in population as possible. They must also not be created to unduly favor any person or political party.

question: Are the current Delaware state legislative maps operative for the 2026 cycle?
question_variants: Status of Delaware Senate redistricting maps
Are there pending redistricting lawsuits in Delaware?
Delaware 2026 state legislative boundaries
answer: Yes. The redistricting maps enacted by the Delaware General Assembly following the 2020 census remain fully operative for the 2026 election cycle. There are no ongoing systemic legal challenges preventing their use.

question: Does Delaware's constitutional amendment process affect the creation of an independent redistricting commission?
question_variants: Can Delaware create an independent redistricting commission by ballot measure?
Constitutional barriers to redistricting reform in DE
How to change redistricting rules in Delaware
answer: Yes, heavily. Because Delaware is the only state where the constitution is amended strictly by a two-thirds vote of the legislature across two successive sessions (without any voter referendum), citizens cannot bypass the legislature to create an independent redistricting commission. The legislature must voluntarily vote to strip itself of mapping power.

question: Does Delaware have a recall mechanism for state officials?
question_variants: Can the Governor of Delaware be recalled?
How to recall a state senator in DE
Statewide recall election rules Delaware
answer: No. The State of Delaware does not have any constitutional or statutory provision allowing for the recall of state-level elected officials, including the Governor, statewide executives, or members of the General Assembly.

question: What are the rules for local recall in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware county council recall rules
Signature requirement local recall Delaware
Recalling a mayor in DE
answer: There is no statewide, general statutory right to recall local officials in Delaware. Recall is highly restricted and is only permitted in a select few municipalities if their specific, state-approved local city charter explicitly authorizes the recall process.

question: Does Delaware have a citizen initiative process?
question_variants: How to get an initiative on the DE ballot
Delaware citizen initiative signature requirements
Can citizens propose laws in Delaware?
answer: No. Delaware is one of the states that does NOT have a statewide citizen initiative process. Citizens cannot draft statutes or constitutional amendments and gather signatures to place them on the ballot.
source_name: Ballotpedia - Initiative and Referendum in Delaware

question: Does Delaware have a veto referendum process?
question_variants: How to refer a DE bill to the ballot
Delaware citizen referendum rules
Veto referendum signature threshold Delaware state
answer: No. Delaware does not have a statewide veto referendum process. Citizens cannot gather signatures to suspend and refer an already-passed legislative act to the ballot for a popular vote of approval or rejection.
source_name: Ballotpedia - Initiative and Referendum in Delaware

question: How does Delaware refer a constitutional amendment to the voters?
question_variants: Legislative referral process Delaware
Referendum by legislature DE
Can the DE legislature put a constitutional amendment on the ballot?
answer: In the most distinctive constitutional feature in the nation, Delaware NEVER refers constitutional amendments to the voters. Under Article IX of the Delaware Constitution, the General Assembly amends the constitution by securing a two-thirds vote in two consecutive legislative sessions, with an election intervening. Voter ratification is not required.

question: Is there an elected Comptroller in Delaware?
question_variants: Requirements to run for DE Comptroller
Who can run for Comptroller in DE?
Delaware Comptroller qualifications
answer: No. Delaware does not have the office of an elected Comptroller. The state's financial administration and oversight are divided between the elected State Treasurer and the elected Auditor of Accounts.

question: What are the eligibility requirements to run for State Treasurer in Delaware?
question_variants: Qualifications for Delaware State Treasurer
Running for Treasurer in DE
Who elects the State Treasurer in Delaware?
answer: The Delaware State Treasurer is elected by the voters statewide. There are no specific enumerated constitutional age or residency requirements beyond being a qualified elector (registered voter) and citizen of Delaware.

question: What are the eligibility requirements to run for Auditor of Accounts in Delaware?
question_variants: Qualifications for Delaware State Auditor
Running for Auditor in DE
Who elects the Auditor in Delaware?
answer: The Delaware Auditor of Accounts is a constitutional executive office elected statewide for a four-year term. Similar to the Treasurer, there are no specific constitutional qualifications (such as holding a CPA license) required to run for the office beyond being a qualified elector of the state.

question: What are the eligibility requirements for a school board candidate in Delaware?
question_variants: Run for school board Delaware requirements
Who can be a school board member in DE?
School board residency rules Delaware
answer: To run for a local school board in Delaware, a candidate must be a citizen of the state and a resident of the specific school district they seek to represent. Candidates who are registered sex offenders or who have been convicted of certain disqualifying felonies are ineligible.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - School Boards

question: What are the qualifications for justices of the Delaware Supreme Court?
question_variants: Delaware Supreme Court Justice eligibility
Years of practice required for DE Supreme Court
Are Delaware high court judges elected or appointed?
answer: Delaware Supreme Court justices are NOT elected. They are appointed by the Governor and confirmed by the State Senate for 12-year terms. They must be citizens of the state and learned in the law. Uniquely, the Delaware Constitution dictates strict political balance on the courts (no more than a bare majority may be from the same political party).

question: What is the ballot signature cure process for absentee mail-in ballots in Delaware?
question_variants: How to fix a rejected signature in DE
Delaware ballot cure process deadline
Missing signature on DE mail ballot
answer: Delaware historically lacked an absentee ballot cure process. However, recent legislative updates (codified via SB 266 in 2026) formally require the Department of Elections to notify voters of certain deficiencies (like a missing signature) on their ballot envelope and offer the voter an opportunity to cure the error before the polls close on Election Day.

question: What are the deadlines for military and overseas (UOCAVA) voters in Delaware?
question_variants: When are military ballots due in Delaware?
UOCAVA return deadline DE
Overseas voting rules Delaware state
answer: Under UOCAVA, ballots are transmitted to military and overseas voters at least 45 days before an election. In Delaware, voted UOCAVA absentee ballots must be received by the Department of Elections no later than the close of polls (8:00 p.m.) on Election Day to be counted.

question: When are provisional ballots used in Delaware?
question_variants: What is a provisional ballot in DE?
Voting without ID Delaware
When do election workers issue provisional ballots?
answer: Provisional ballots are issued if a voter's name is not on the official poll list but they affirm they are registered and eligible to vote in that district. The voter signs an affidavit on the provisional envelope. Delaware county election officials review the voter's status post-election to determine if the provisional ballot will be tabulated.

question: Does Delaware have Automatic Voter Registration (AVR) at the DMV?
question_variants: Is registration automatic at the Delaware DMV?
DE motor voter automatic registration
Do I have to register to vote if I get a DE driver's license?
answer: Yes. Delaware implemented Automatic Voter Registration (AVR) in 2021. Eligible individuals conducting business at the Division of Motor Vehicles are automatically registered to vote or have their address updated, unless they actively decline (opt-out) during the transaction.

question: Does Delaware offer Online Voter Registration?
question_variants: Can I register to vote online in DE?
Delaware electronic voter registration
DE ivote online registration portal
answer: Yes. Eligible Delaware residents with a valid state driver's license or DMV-issued ID card can register to vote or update their registration securely using the state's iVote.de.gov online portal.

question: How does Delaware maintain its voter registration lists?
question_variants: DE voter list maintenance rules
How are inactive voters handled in Delaware?
Delaware voter roll cleaning process
answer: Delaware maintains its voter rolls using the Electronic Registration Information Center (ERIC) to track voters who have moved or died. The Department of Elections also utilizes National Change of Address (NCOA) data. Voters who do not respond to address confirmation notices and fail to vote in two consecutive federal general elections are removed from the active registry.

question: What is the deadline to return an absentee ballot in Delaware?
question_variants: When is the last day to mail my ballot in DE?
Delaware mail ballot postmark deadline
What time are ballots due in Delaware?
answer: Delaware strictly requires that all voted absentee ballots be physically RECEIVED by the county Department of Elections office by the close of polls (8:00 p.m.) on Election Day. Ballots arriving after 8:00 p.m. on Election Day are rejected, regardless of postmark date. They cannot be returned at Early Voting Sites or standard polling places.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Absentee Application

question: Is Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) used in Delaware?
question_variants: Does Delaware use ranked choice voting?
RCV status in Delaware 2026
Instant runoff voting DE
answer: No. Ranked Choice Voting (RCV) is not used for any state, federal, or widespread local elections in Delaware. The state relies strictly on the traditional primary and plurality general election system.

question: How are exact ties broken in Delaware elections?
question_variants: How are tied elections decided in Delaware?
Tie-breaker rules Delaware elections
What happens if a race is tied in DE?
answer: In the highly unusual event of an exact tie in a general election for a county or statewide office, the Superior Court, sitting as the board of canvass, certifies the tie to the Governor. The Governor is then required to call a special election to resolve the tie.

question: What disclaimer language is required on Delaware campaign materials?
question_variants: Delaware 'Paid for by' rules
Campaign ad disclaimer requirements DE
Political taglines Delaware CFRS
answer: Delaware campaign finance law requires that all printed political materials and broadcast advertisements clearly indicate the individual or committee responsible for funding the communication. The disclaimer must clearly state 'Paid for by' followed by the name of the candidate committee or PAC.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Campaign Finance

question: When does outside spending become prohibited coordination in Delaware?
question_variants: DE campaign coordination rules
When is a PAC expenditure not independent in Delaware?
Prohibition on candidate-PAC coordination DE
answer: An independent expenditure becomes 'coordinated' if it is made in cooperation, consultation, or concert with, or at the request or suggestion of, a candidate or their authorized campaign committee. Coordinated expenditures are legally treated as in-kind contributions and are subject to Delaware's strict per-election contribution limits.

question: What are the penalties for late campaign finance filings in Delaware?
question_variants: DE CFRS late fee
Penalty for missing a Delaware campaign finance deadline
State Election Commissioner fine for late filing DE
answer: The State Election Commissioner assesses mandatory administrative penalties for tardy campaign finance reports. The fine is $50 per day for each day the report is late, up to a maximum of 100 days ($5,000). Candidates may be prohibited from running in future elections if fines are unpaid.

question: When and how must a Delaware campaign committee terminate?
question_variants: Closing a Delaware campaign committee
CFRS committee termination requirements
Final campaign finance report Delaware
answer: A candidate committee may formally terminate when it will no longer receive contributions or make expenditures. The committee must achieve a zero cash balance, legally dispose of all surplus funds, and file a formal Final Report marking the campaign account as closed within the CFRS portal.

question: What are the disclosure rules for independent expenditures in Delaware?
question_variants: DE independent expenditure reporting CFRS
Disclosure for DE independent expenditure groups
Reporting thresholds for outside spending Delaware
answer: Organizations making independent expenditures must file reports with the State Election Commissioner. The reports must be filed within a standard reporting schedule, except that any independent expenditure exceeding $250 made within 9 days of an election triggers a mandatory 24-hour rapid disclosure report.

question: How are in-kind contributions valued and reported in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware in-kind contribution valuation rules
Reporting non-monetary donations Delaware campaign
Fair market value for in-kind gifts DE
answer: In-kind contributions (donated goods, services, or equipment not available to the general public) must be reported on CFRS disclosures at their fair market value. They count directly toward the donor's per-election period contribution limit ($1,200 or $600).

question: Is there a session freeze on campaign contributions for Delaware legislators?
question_variants: Can Delaware legislators raise money during session?
Lobbyist contribution ban DE legislature
Legislative session contribution rules Delaware
answer: No. Unlike some other states, Delaware does not have a statutory 'session freeze' that prohibits legislators or statewide officials from accepting campaign contributions while the General Assembly is in regular session.

question: What can Delaware candidates do with unexpended campaign funds?
question_variants: What can I do with leftover campaign money in DE?
DE surplus campaign funds rules
Donating remaining campaign funds Delaware
answer: Unexpended campaign funds cannot be converted to personal use. Candidates may retain surplus funds for a future campaign, return them pro-rata to contributors, transfer them to a recognized political party, or donate them to an IRS-recognized tax-exempt charitable organization.

question: What are Delaware's rapid disclosure requirements for late contributions?
question_variants: Delaware 24-hour large contribution report
Late contribution reporting Delaware CFRS
When do I file a rapid report in DE?
answer: Third-party groups making independent expenditures must file a 24-hour rapid disclosure report if they make an expenditure exceeding $250 within 9 days of the election, or if they receive a single contribution over $2,000 at any time. Candidates generally adhere to the strict 8-Day Pre-Election report.

question: What are the terms and election cycles for county offices in Delaware?
question_variants: When are county sheriffs elected in DE?
Delaware county official term lengths
Are county councils on the 2026 ballot?
answer: Elected county officials across Delaware's three counties—including County Executives, Councilmembers, Levy Court Commissioners, and Sheriffs—generally serve four-year staggered terms. Because they are aligned with the presidential and midterm cycles, numerous county offices will be featured on the 2026 ballot.

question: What is the canvassing and certification timeline for Delaware elections?
question_variants: When are Delaware election results official?
DE certification deadline
Superior Court Board of Canvass meeting Delaware
answer: In Delaware, the Superior Court in each county sits as the Board of Canvass. They convene publicly on the Thursday following the Tuesday election (2 days later) to officially calculate the votes, resolve disputes, and certify the name of the winning candidates, forwarding the results to the Governor.

question: When and how are special elections called in Delaware?
question_variants: Delaware special election timeline
Who calls a special election to fill a US House vacancy in DE?
Filling a midterm vacancy Delaware
answer: Special elections to fill vacancies in the General Assembly or federal offices are called by a Writ of Election issued by the presiding officer of the respective chamber or the Governor. The Department of Elections then administers the special election within a statutorily defined window.

question: What is the role of the State Election Commissioner in Delaware?
question_variants: Who oversees elections in DE?
Delaware Department of Elections structure
State Election Commissioner duties Delaware
answer: The State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Governor and confirmed by the Senate. They oversee the Department of Elections, ensure uniformity in the administration of election laws across all three counties, manage the state voter registration database, and enforce campaign finance regulations.

question: What are the specific dates for early in-person voting in Delaware for 2026?
question_variants: When is early voting for the Delaware primary 2026?
DE early voting dates 2026 general election
How many days of early voting in Delaware?
answer: For the 2026 cycle, early voting for the primary election is tentatively scheduled from September 2 to September 13 (with no voting on Labor Day, Sept 6-7). For the November 3 general election, early voting is scheduled from October 22 to November 1 (with polls closed on Oct 25). Polls are open daily during these windows.
source_name: Delaware Department of Elections - Early Voting Sites
